events.source_quote: In the fourth quarter of 2015, Robert H. Eder (the Company’s Chairman and Chief Executive Officer) and Frank K. Rogers (the Company’s Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer) met with one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”) to discuss joint commercial opportunities. During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company to further develop strategic opportunities and enhance regional connectivity.

events.bid_date_rough: shortly after Q4 2015 Party A meeting
events.source_quote: Upon receiving a report of this meeting, in recognition of its public company obligations, the Board appointed a subcommittee of two independent directors, Frank W. Barrett and Richard W. Anderson, to interview potential investment bankers to represent the Company in possible negotiations with Party A and to assist the Board in evaluating additional strategic options for the realization of shareholder value, including potential change in control transactions.

flags.reason: anchor: Q4 2015 Party A meeting (2015-11-15) + 7 days per §B1 'upon/shortly after' offset

events.additional_note: Board subcommittee appointed to evaluate potential change in control transactions

events.source_quote: During the executive session of the Board’s regular quarterly meeting held on January 27, 2016 (which Director Alfred P. Smith did not attend), the subcommittee recommended to the Board that the Company retain GHF as its investment banking firm. At that meeting, representatives of GHF discussed with the Board its qualifications and a proposed process should the directors ultimately decide to proceed with a change in control transaction. As part of the interview process, GHF represented to the Company that it would not represent any potential buyer of the Company in connection with an offer to purchase the Company or any person proposing to provide financing in connection with such an offer. Following this presentation, the Board approved the subcommittee’s recommendation and the Company engaged GHF.
events.additional_note: GHF subsequently acquired by BMO Capital Markets Corp. on 2016-08-01 per later Background narration; same advisor relationship continued

events.source_quote: During the week of March 28, 2016, in accordance with the Transaction Committee’s directives, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers. Each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements.

the Board met in executive session (without Mr. Eder present) with representatives of GHF and discussed the status of the process, including that each potential buyer had been advised to submit a non-binding indication of interest by May 10, 2016 (which deadline was subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016 to ensure the potential buyers had sufficient time to formulate their most compelling offers).
events.additional_note: Initial IOI deadline May 10, 2016 postponed to May 19, 2016. Transaction Committee communicated informal round deadline to NDA signers.

flags.reason: no explicit 'final round' phrase; inferred from subset-invitation-to-submit language per §K2

events.source_quote: Between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
events.additional_note: one of nine written IOIs; aggregate price range $17.93-$26.50 per share across all nine

flags.reason: Filing gives aggregate IOI range $17.93-$26.50 across 9 bids but not per-bidder prices

events.source_quote: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process. The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence.
events.additional_note: one of two lowest IOI bidders excluded by Transaction Committee on 2016-06-01

flags.reason: Filing states two low IOI bidders excluded by Transaction Committee; emitting 2 placeholder DropBelowInf rows (§E5)

events.source_quote: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process. The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence.
events.additional_note: one of two lowest IOI bidders excluded by Transaction Committee on 2016-06-01

events.source_quote: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site.
events.additional_note: GHF instructed all NDA signers to submit non-binding LOIs by July 20, 2016 with markups of merger agreement, disclosure letter, and voting agreement

flags.reason: no explicit 'final round' phrase; inferred from process-letter-like instruction to submit LOIs by deadline

events.source_quote: In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company. After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers.
events.additional_note: Party C was NOT among the 25 initial NDA signers of Mar 28; joined later

events.source_quote: On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million). Subsequently, Party C was provided access to the internet data site and the Company’s management held a management presentation with Party C by telephone on July 14, 2016.
events.additional_note: IOI; implies equity value of $108 million
events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: filing calls this a non-binding IOI; pre-LOI stage

source_quote: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million). Party B, G&W and another bidder (“Party D”) also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement. One bidder (“Party E”) provided a summary of material issues and proposed changes to the merger agreement and voting agreement with respect to those issues.

Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share (subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation).
events.additional_note: 60-day exclusivity; summary of material issues and proposed merger-agreement changes (not full markup)
events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: 'non-binding LOI' + no merger-agreement markup (only 'summary of material issues'); process position Final Round Inf Ann defaults informal

flags.reason: phrase: 'late July 2016'; filing LOI deadline July 20, 2016

Party C submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.30 per share (subject to a 30-day diligence period).
events.additional_note: 30-day diligence; no merger-agreement markup narrated
events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: 'non-binding LOI' + no merger-agreement markup narrated; process-position default Final Round Inf Ann → informal

Party B, G&W and another bidder (“Party D”) also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement. One bidder (“Party E”) provided a summary of material issues and proposed changes to the merger agreement and voting agreement with respect to those issues.
Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review).
events.additional_note: LOI with markup of merger agreement, voting agreement; expedited diligence review
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: 'markup of merger agreement' is an explicit formal trigger (overrides base Final Round Inf Ann informal default)

flags.reason: phrase: 'late July 2016'; filing LOI deadline July 20, 2016; anchored on deadline

source_quote: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million). Party B, G&W and another bidder (“Party D”) also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement. One bidder (“Party E”) provided a summary of material issues and proposed changes to the merger agreement and voting agreement with respect to those issues.
Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share (subject to a four-week diligence period).
events.additional_note: 4-week diligence; LOI with markup of merger agreement, voting agreement
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: 'markup of merger agreement' is an explicit formal trigger (Party D provided markups per p.36 text)

flags.reason: phrase: 'late July 2016'; filing LOI deadline July 20, 2016

source_quote: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million). Party B, G&W and another bidder (“Party D”) also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement. One bidder (“Party E”) provided a summary of material issues and proposed changes to the merger agreement and voting agreement with respect to those issues.
One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.
events.additional_note: LOI submission deadline. Six LOIs received (Party B, G&W, Party E, Party D, Party C, Party F). One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit.

events.source_quote: G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share (subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period). The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right, the value of which was based on the subsequent sale of the Company’s South Quay property (“CVR”). G&W also provided a mark-up of the disclosure letter.
events.additional_note: 3-week exclusive diligence; includes CVR based on future sale of South Quay property; markup of disclosure letter
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: 'markup' signal (disclosure letter markup + LOI including specific consideration structure)

events.source_quote: On July 26, 2016, in response to feedback from representatives of GHF indicating its price and CVR structure were not competitive, G&W submitted a revised LOI, which increased its offer to $22.15 per share (including $21.02 in cash and $1.13 CVR).
events.additional_note: Revised LOI up from $21.15 to $22.15 per share; price and CVR structure
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: continuation of G&W’s July 21 formal LOI (markup trigger persists)

events.source_quote: The Transaction Committee concluded that the Company should proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B because of the higher offers made by each of G&W and Party B relative to the other potential buyers. At the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF subsequently contacted the remaining bidders to inform them that they were no longer involved in the process.
events.additional_note: Transaction Committee selects G&W and Party B for confirmatory diligence and negotiation of merger agreement. Per §K2 this is the final-round invitation to the subset.

flags.reason: no explicit 'final round' phrase; inferred from subset-invitation-to-continue language per §K2

At the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF subsequently contacted the remaining bidders to inform them that they were no longer involved in the process.
events.additional_note: Party C informed they were no longer involved in the process after Transaction Committee selected G&W and Party B

At the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF subsequently contacted the remaining bidders to inform them that they were no longer involved in the process.
events.additional_note: Party F informed they were no longer involved in the process after Transaction Committee selected G&W and Party B

source_quote: On July 29, 2016, representatives of GHF had calls with Party D and Party E. During those calls, Party D and Party E both expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing their offers.

On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.
events.additional_note: Reengagement after July 27 DropTarget; revised LOI $24/share with 30-day diligence period
events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: post-LOI revised non-binding proposal; no new markup signal narrated; filing later describes target declining commitment to Party D

flags.reason: Party D previously dropped at July 27 Transaction Committee cut; re-engaged July 29, submitted revised LOI Aug 1

events.source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.
events.additional_note: Reengagement revised LOI; financing support from Party F; 30-day diligence. Withdrawn on Aug 2.
events.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: post-LOI revised non-binding proposal; no new markup signal narrated

flags.reason: Party E previously dropped at July 27 Transaction Committee cut; re-engaged July 29, submitted revised LOI Aug 1 with Party F financing

events.source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share) and Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.
events.additional_note: Withdrew revised $23.81 proposal; filing notes original $21.26 LOI still stood at this moment though target later prioritized G&W and Party B

events.source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share) and Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.
events.additional_note: Company declined Party D’s requested commitment not to enter agreement with another bidder during its 30-day DD; Party D indicated it would not proceed with further due diligence

events.source_quote: On the morning of August 12, 2016, following a discussion between representatives of BMO and G&W in which G&W indicated it would be submitting a revised LOI, G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and the voting agreement. G&W’s LOI further indicated that the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.
events.additional_note: Revised LOI $25 all-cash (CVR excluded) with markups of merger agreement and voting agreement; offer expiring 6:00 p.m. Aug 13. 53% premium over prior-day stock close.
events.bid_type_inference_note: formal per §G1: 'markups of the merger agreement' explicit formal trigger; offer has explicit expiration window

events.source_quote: At the request of the Board, representatives of BMO then contacted Party B to determine if it would increase its offer price. Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.
events.additional_note: BMO contacted Party B at Board's request; Party B indicated it would not increase price

events.source_quote: The Board determined that the G&W offer represented the superior proposal. At the request of the Board, representatives of BMO reviewed BMO’s financial analyses supporting its opinion to the Board as to the fairness, from a financial point of view, to the holders of our common stock of the consideration to be received by those holders (other than the Eder Trusts) in the merger pursuant to the merger agreement as of August 12, 2016.
events.additional_note: Board determined G&W offer represented superior proposal after Party B declined to increase. No publicly-announced final-round deadline; target unilaterally closed bidding.

source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher finalized the transaction documents and the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement. The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016 prior to the opening of trading of the Company’s common stock on the NASDAQ.

At the effective time of the merger, each share of common stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the merger (including any preferred stock deemed to be converted into common stock, but excluding shares that are owned by G&W or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries, including Merger Sub) will be canceled and will be converted automatically into the right to receive $25.00 in cash, without interest.

Hinckley Allen advised the Board that both Party B’s and G&W’s draft merger agreements restricted the Company from soliciting a competing proposal, subject to a “fiduciary out” for an unsolicited superior proposal, which required the Company to pay a termination fee equal to 3% of the transaction value in the case of G&W (which the Company had negotiated down from 3.85%) and 3% plus Party B’s transaction expenses (capped at 1% of transaction value) in the case of Party B, in the event the merger agreement was terminated as a result of the Board’s decision to pursue such a proposal.
events.additional_note: Merger agreement executed Aug 12, 2016; public announcement Aug 15, 2016. $25.00/share in cash. Termination fee 3% of transaction value.
